```
        $stmt->execute([$userId]);
        $dates = $stmt->fetchAll(\PDO::FETCH_COLUMN);

        if (empty($dates)) return 0;

        $streak = 0;
        $today = new \DateTime();
        $checkDate = clone $today;

        foreach ($dates as $dateStr) {
            $date = new \DateTime($dateStr);
            $diff = (int) $checkDate->diff($date)->days;

            if ($streak === 0) {
                if ($diff <= 1) {
                    $streak = 1;
                    $checkDate = $date;
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            } else {
                if ($diff === 1) {
                    $streak++;
                    $checkDate = $date;
                } else {
                    break;
                }
            }
        }

        return $streak;
    }

        self::ensureHistoryTable();
```

```
        $correctCount = (int) $correct->fetchColumn();

        $daily = $pdo->prepare("

            WHERE user_id = ? AND reviewed_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)

        ");

        $stmt->execute([$userId]);
        $learnedCount = (int) $stmt->fetchColumn();

        $progress = $totalCardCount > 0 ? (int) round(($learnedCount / $totalCardCount) * 100) : 0;

        return [
            'total_reviews' => $totalReviews,
            'correct_count' => $correctCount,

            'daily' => $dailyStats,
            'due_today' => $dueToday,
            'streak_days' => $streak,
            'learned_count' => $learnedCount,
            'total_cards' => $totalCardCount,
            'progress' => $progress,
        ];
    }
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/leonardo/Documents/Personal/Inglês/Aulas/materialLeo/ankiCards/flashcards/src/Review.php",
  "oldString": "    public static function getStats(int $userId): array\n    {\n        self::ensureHistoryTable();\n        $pdo = Database::getConnection();\n\n        $total = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?\");\n        $total->execute([$userId]);\n        $totalReviews = (int) $total->fetchColumn();\n\n        $correct = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ? AND was_correct = 1\");\n        $correct->execute([$userId]);\n        $correctCount = (int) $correct->fetchColumn();\n\n        $distinctCards = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT card_id) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?\");\n        $distinctCards->execute([$userId]);\n        $cardsReviewed = (int) $distinctCards->fetchColumn();\n\n        $daily = $pdo->prepare(\"\n            SELECT DATE(reviewed_at) as day, COUNT(*) as count\n            FROM review_history\n            WHERE user_id = ? AND reviewed_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)\n            GROUP BY DATE(reviewed_at)\n            ORDER BY day ASC\n        \");\n        $daily->execute([$userId]);\n        $dailyStats = $daily->fetchAll();\n\n        $upcoming = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_card_progress WHERE user_id = ? AND next_review <= CURDATE()\");\n        $upcoming->execute([$userId]);\n        $dueToday = (int) $upcoming->fetchColumn();\n\n        return [\n            'total_reviews' => $totalReviews,\n            'correct_count' => $correctCount,\n            'incorrect_count' => $totalReviews - $correctCount,\n            'cards_reviewed' => $cardsReviewed,\n            'daily' => $dailyStats,\n            'due_today' => $dueToday,\n        ];\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "    public static function getStreakDays(int $userId): int\n    {\n        self::ensureHistoryTable();\n        $pdo = Database::getConnection();\n\n        $stmt = $pdo->prepare(\"\n            SELECT DISTINCT DATE(reviewed_at) as review_date\n            FROM review_history\n            WHERE user_id = ?\n            ORDER BY review_date DESC\n        \");\n        $stmt->execute([$userId]);\n        $dates = $stmt->fetchAll(\\PDO::FETCH_COLUMN);\n\n        if (empty($dates)) return 0;\n\n        $streak = 0;\n        $today = new \\DateTime();\n        $checkDate = clone $today;\n\n        foreach ($dates as $dateStr) {\n            $date = new \\DateTime($dateStr);\n            $diff = (int) $checkDate->diff($date)->days;\n\n            if ($streak === 0) {\n                if ($diff <= 1) {\n                    $streak = 1;\n                    $checkDate = $date;\n                } else {\n                    break;\n                }\n            } else {\n                if ($diff === 1) {\n                    $streak++;\n                    $checkDate = $date;\n                } else {\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return $streak;\n    }\n\n    public static function getStats(int $userId): array\n    {\n        self::ensureHistoryTable();\n        $pdo = Database::getConnection();\n\n        $total = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?\");\n        $total->execute([$userId]);\n        $totalReviews = (int) $total->fetchColumn();\n\n        $correct = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ? AND was_correct = 1\");\n        $correct->execute([$userId]);\n        $correctCount = (int) $correct->fetchColumn();\n\n        $distinctCards = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT card_id) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?\");\n        $distinctCards->execute([$userId]);\n        $cardsReviewed = (int) $distinctCards->fetchColumn();\n\n        $daily = $pdo->prepare(\"\n            SELECT DATE(reviewed_at) as day, COUNT(*) as count\n            FROM review_history\n            WHERE user_id = ? AND reviewed_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)\n            GROUP BY DATE(reviewed_at)\n            ORDER BY day ASC\n        \");\n        $daily->execute([$userId]);\n        $dailyStats = $daily->fetchAll();\n\n        $upcoming = $pdo->prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_card_progress WHERE user_id = ? AND next_review <= CURDATE()\");\n        $upcoming->execute([$userId]);\n        $dueToday = (int) $upcoming->fetchColumn();\n\n        $streak = self::getStreakDays($userId);\n\n        return [\n            'total_reviews' => $totalReviews,\n            'correct_count' => $correctCount,\n            'incorrect_count' => $totalReviews - $correctCount,\n            'cards_reviewed' => $cardsReviewed,\n            'daily' => $dailyStats,\n            'due_today' => $dueToday,\n            'streak_days' => $streak,\n        ];\n    }\n}"
}
```

Now let me think about what the cardinspector needs:

1. Standalone PHP file (cardinspector.php) 
2. Admin password protection - check admin credentials
3. Opens a CSV file like the import module
4. Left side: scrollable list showing cardset - cardname - cardstyle
5. Center: editable fields based on card style, dynamically changing when style changes
6. Two buttons: Save to CSV (for later import) OR Import to database

Let me check if there's an existing pattern for admin authentication in standalone PHP files that I can follow.

Actually, looking at the user's description more carefully:
- It should "open the csv just like the import module" - meaning the file upload/drop zone
- Left panel: scrollable list of cards from CSV (cardset, cardname, cardstyle)
- Center panel: editable fields that change based on card style
- Two action buttons: Save CSV (export modifications back to CSV) OR Import to DB

For the admin check, I can use the same auth mechanism as admin_cards.php - session-based with admin login.

But wait, the user said "must provide admin password to enter" - this means a simple password gate, not a full login system. Let me make it simple: a password form that checks against the admin credentials.

Actually, let me keep it simple and consistent with the existing codebase. I'll use the same session-based auth as admin_cards.php.

Let me look at how the import module works to understand the data flow:
1. User drops/selects a CSV file
2. CSV is sent to `admin_cards.php?action=preview_csv` which parses it and returns JSON
3. The cards are displayed in a table with inline editing
4. User can then import to DB via `api/import_csv.php`

For the cardinspector, I need:
1. Same CSV parsing
2. A side-by-side layout (left list, center editor)
3. Dynamic field rendering based on card style
4. Save to CSV (download modified CSV)
5. Import to database

Let me also look at how the editor fields are rendered in admin_cards.php to reuse the field structures.

Let me first understand the card types and their fields:

1. `usage_cases` - definition, usage1, tip, example1, example2
2. `deep_dive` - definition, usage1, tip, example1, example2
3. `formula_table` - definition, example
4. `multiple_choice` - question_text, options (opt1-opt4), correct_index, explanation
5. `gap_fill` - sentence, correct_answers, example
6. `image_mcq` - image_url, question_text, options (opt1-opt4), correct_index, explanation
7. `image_description` - image_url, description
8. `audio_listening` - audio_url, prompt, correct_answers, transcript

I'll build this as a standalone PHP file that:
1. Has a password gate
2. Has a CSV upload area
3. Shows a split view (list + editor)
4. Has dynamic fields based on style
5. Save to CSV + Import to DB

Let me build this file. It's going to be fairly large but self-contained.

const levelMap = {\n                'beginner': 'Beginner', 'a1': 'Beginner', 'a2': 'Beginner',\n                'intermediate': 'Intermediate', 'b1': 'Intermediate', 'b2': 'Intermediate',\n                'advanced': 'Advanced', 'c1': 'Advanced', 'c2': 'Advanced',\n            };\n            let level = (r.level || 'Beginner').trim();\n            level = levelMap[level.toLowerCase()] || level;\n 'selected' : ''}>${v}</option>`).join('')}\n                    </select>\n                </div>\n                <div>\n                    <label class=\"field-label\">Level</label>\n                    <select id=\"editLevel\" class=\"form-select\">\n                        <option value=\"Beginner\" ${row.level === 'Beginner' ? class=\"btn btn-danger btn-sm\">Remove</button>\n            </div>\n        `;\n        editorPanel.innerHTML = html;\n\n        document.getElementById('editType').addEventListener('change', () => {\n            const newType = document.getElementById('editType').value;\n            const fieldsDiv = document.getElementById('dynamicFields');\n            const dummyRow = { ...row, type: newType };\n            fieldsDiv.innerHTML = renderDynamicFields(dummyRow);\n        });\n\n        document.getElementById('applyCardBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            applyEditorToRow(selectedIdx);\n            renderList();\n            if (selectedIdx >= 0) selectCard(selectedIdx);\n        });\n\n        document.getElementById('deleteCardBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!confirm('Remove this card from the import list?')) return;\n            rows.splice(selectedIdx, 1);\n {\n        const type = row.type || 'usage_cases';\n        const cd = {};\n        // Parse content_data if it exists as a JSON string\n        let contentData = row.content_data;\n        if (typeof <div><label class=\"field-label\">Description</label><textarea id=\"editDescription\" class=\"form-textarea\" rows=\"5\">${escapeHtml(description)}</textarea></div>\n            `;\n        }\n        if (type === 'audio_listening') {\n            return `\n                <div><label class=\"field-label\">Audio URL</label><input type=\"text\" id=\"editAudioUrl\" class=\"form-input\" row.definition = '';\n            row.usage1 = '';\n            row.tip = '';\n            row.image_url = type === 'image_mcq' ? (document.getElementById('editImageUrl')?.value || '') : '';\n            row.explanation = document.getElementById('editExplanation')?.value || '';\n            // Store in content_data as CSV columns\n            row['opt1'] = opts.split(',')[0]?.trim() || '';\n            row['opt2'] = opts.split(',')[1]?.trim() || '';\n            row['opt3'] = opts.split(',')[2]?.trim() || '';\n            row['opt4'] = opts.split(',')[3]?.trim() || '';\n            row.correct_answer = String(row.correct_index);\n        } else if (type === 'gap_fill') {\n            row.sentence = document.getElementById('editSentence')?.value || '';\n            const answers = document.getElementById('editCorrectAnswers')?.value || 'answer';\n            row.correct_answer = answers.split(',').map(s => s.trim()).join(',');\n            row.example1 = document.getElementById('editExample')?.value || '';\n            row.definition = '';\n            row.usage1 = '';\n            row.tip = '';\n        } else if (type === 'image_description') {\n            row.image_url = document.getElementById('editImageUrl')?.value || '';\n            row.description = document.getElementById('editDescription')?.value || '';\n            row.definition = row.description;\n            row.usage1 = '';\n            row.tip = '';\n        } else if (type === 'audio_listening') {\n            row.audio_url = document.getElementById('editAudioUrl')?.value || '';\n            row.prompt = document.getElementById('editPrompt')?.value || '';\n            const answers = document.getElementById('editCorrectAnswers')?.value || '';\n            row.correct_answer = answers;\n            row.transcript = document.getElementById('editNotes')?.value || '';\n            row.definition = '';\n            row.usage1 = '';\n            row.tip = '';\n        } else {\n            row.image_url = document.getElementById('editImageUrl')?.value || '';\n            row.audio_url = document.getElementById('editAudioUrl')?.value || '';\n            row.definition = document.getElementById('editDefinition')?.value || '';\n            row.usage1 = document.getElementById('editUsage')?.value || '';\n            row.tip = document.getElementById('editTip')?.value || '';\n            row.question_text = '';\n            row.sentence = '';\n            row.correct_answer = '';\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Export CSV ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n    function buildCsvContent() {\n        const cols = ['set','set_id','type','title','level','definition','question_text','sentence','example1','example2','usage1','tip','correct_answer','explanation','image_url','audio_url','description','prompt','transcript'];\n        const lines = [cols.join(',')];\n        rows.forEach(row => {\n            const vals = cols.map(col => {\n                let val = '';\n                if (col === 'set') val = row._setName || '';\n                else if (col === 'set_id') val = row.set_id || '';\n                else if (col === 'type') val = row.type || 'usage_cases';\n                else if (col === 'level') val = row.level || 'Beginner';\n                else if (col === 'title') val = row.title || '';\n                else if (col === 'definition') val = row.definition || '';\n                else if (col === 'question_text') val = row.question_text || '';\n                else if (col === 'sentence') val = row.sentence || '';\n                else if (col === 'example1') val = row.example1 || '';\n                else if (col === 'example2') val = row.example2 || '';\n                else if (col === 'usage1') val = row.usage1 || '';\n                else if (col === 'tip') val = row.tip || '';\n                else if (col === 'correct_answer') val = row.correct_answer || '';\n                else if (col === 'explanation') val = row.explanation || '';\n                else if (col === 'image_url') val = row.image_url || '';\n                else if (col === 'audio_url') val = row.audio_url || '';\n                else if (col === 'description') val = row.description || '';\n                else if (col === 'prompt') val = row.prompt || '';\n                else if (col === 'transcript') val = row.transcript || '';\n                else val = row[col] || '';\n                if (val.includes(',') || val.includes('\"') || val.includes('\\n')) val = '\"' + val.replace(/\"/g, '\"\"') + '\"';\n                return val;\n            });\n            lines.push(vals.join(','));\n        });\n        return lines.join('\\n');\n    }\n\n    document.getElementById('saveCsvBtn')?.addEventListener('click', () => {\n        if (rows.length === 0) { alert('No rows to save'); return; }\n        applyEditorToRow(selectedIdx);\n        const csv = buildCsvContent();\n        const blob = new Blob([csv], { type: 'text/csv' });\n        const a = document.createElement('a');\n        a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob);\n        a.download = originalFile ? originalFile.name.replace('.csv', '_edited.csv') : 'export.csv';\n        a.click();\n        URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href);\n    });\n\n    document.getElementById('importDbBtn')?.addEventListener('click', async () => {\n        if (rows.length === 0) { alert('No rows to import'); return; }\n        applyEditorToRow(selectedIdx);\n        if (!confirm(`Import ${rows.length} cards into the database?`)) return;\n\n        const csv = buildCsvContent();\n        const blob = new Blob([csv], { type: 'text/csv' });\n        const formData = new FormData();\n        formData.append('csv', blob, (originalFile ? originalFile.name : 'import.csv'));\n\n        try {\n            const res = await fetch('api/import_csv.php', { method: 'POST', body: formData });\n            const result = await res.json();\n            if (result.success) {\n                let msg = `✅ Imported ${result.imported} cards.`;\n                if (result.errors.length) msg += `\\n⚠️ ${result.errors.length} errors:\\n` + result.errors.slice(0, 10).join('\\n');\n                if (result.errors.length > 10) msg += `\\n...and ${result.errors.length - 10} more`;\n                alert(msg);\n            } else {\n                alert('❌ Import failed: ' + (result.error || 'Unknown error'));\n            }\n        } catch (err) {\n            alert('❌ Network error during import');\n        }\n    });\n})();\n</script>\n</body>\n</html>\n"
```json
}
```

**Bugs fixed (commit `ebc319f`):**

```js
async function loadCsvPreview(file) {
    importFileHandle = file;
    importFileName.textContent = file.name;
    importRowCount.textContent = '';
    importRows = [];
    importHeader = [];
    importSelectedIdx = -1;
    importPreviewBody.innerHTML = '<tr><td colspan="6" class="text-center py-4"><div class="loader"></div> Parsing CSV...</td></tr>';
    
    try {
        const formData = new FormData();
        formData.append('csv', file);
        const response = await fetch('admin_cards.php?action=preview_csv&t=' + Date.now(), {
            method: 'POST',
            body: formData,
            headers: { 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' }
        });
        const result = await response.json();
        if (!result.success) {
            alert('❌ ' + (result.error || 'Failed to parse CSV'));
            return;
        }
        importHeader = result.header || [];
        importRows = (result.rows || []).map(row => {
            ...
        });
        importRowCount.textContent = `(${result.total} rows)`;
        renderImportPreview();
        importStepFile.classList.add('hidden');
        importStepPreview.classList.remove('hidden');
    } catch (err) {
        alert('❌ Network error during CSV preview');
    }
}
```

```js
    if (!validTypes.includes(type)) type = 'usage_cases';
```

OK, I've been looking at this code for a while and I can't find an obvious bug. Let me take a step back and think about this differently.

The user deployed the code to production and says "import cards module is not showing the preview". Looking at the flow:

1. User clicks Import button → `openImportModal()` → modal opens with file drop zone
2. User drops a CSV → `loadCsvPreview()` is called
3. The function sends FormData via POST to `admin_cards.php?action=preview_csv`
4. PHP parses CSV and returns JSON with header, rows, total
5. JS renders the preview and switches from step 1 to step 2

If step 3 fails (network error), the catch block alerts "Network error during CSV preview". The user would see this and presumably tell me.
If step 4 fails (invalid JSON), the catch block also alerts.
If step 5 fails (runtime error in `renderImportPreview()` or `.classList.add/remove`), it's UNCAUGHT because `renderImportPreview()` and the step switching are OUTSIDE the try-catch.

Wait, actually, `renderImportPreview()` is INSIDE the try block. Let me look again:

```js
try {
    const response = await fetch(...);
    const result = await response.json();
    if (!result.success) {
        alert(...);
        return;
    }
    importHeader = result.header || [];
    importRows = (result.rows || []).map(row => { ... });
    importRowCount.textContent = `(${result.total} rows)`;
    renderImportPreview();      // <-- INSIDE try block
    importStepFile.classList.add('hidden');
    importStepPreview.classList.remove('hidden');
} catch (err) {
    alert('❌ Network error during CSV preview');
}
```

Yes, both `renderImportPreview()` and the classList operations are inside the try block. So if they throw, the catch block catches it and shows "Network error during CSV preview". The user would see this error.

```php
$row = array_slice(array_map('trim', $row), 0, count($header));
$rows[] = array_combine($header, array_pad($row, count($header), ''));
```

`array_combine` returns `false` if the number of elements doesn't match. `array_slice(..., 0, count($header))` and `array_pad($row, count($header), '')` should ensure they match. But what if `$header` is not an array? No, `$header` is always from `fgetcsv`.

Wait, `array_combine` requires both arrays to have the same number of elements AND both must have at least 1 element. What if `$header` is empty? Line 75-79 handles empty CSV by returning early. What if `$header` has 1 element and `$row` has 0 elements? `array_pad([], 1, '')` returns `['']`, and `array_combine(['header'], [''])` works fine.

OK, what about duplicate header names? `array_combine` doesn't care about duplicates - it just uses the last value.

Let me check something - does `fgetcsv` handle BOM properly? Line 82:
```php
$header[0] = preg_replace('/^\xEF\xBB\xBF/', '', $header[0]);
```
This strips BOM from the first header. Should be fine.

Let me just try something really basic - let me check if the preview_csv endpoint works syntactically:

```php
if ($action === 'preview_csv') {
    if (!isset($_FILES['csv']) || $_FILES['csv']['error'] !== UPLOAD_ERR_OK) {
        echo json_encode(['success' => false, 'error' => 'CSV file required']);
        exit;
    }
    $handle = fopen($_FILES['csv']['tmp_name'], 'r');
    if (!$handle) {
        echo json_encode(['success' => false, 'error' => 'Cannot read file']);
        exit;
    }
    $header = fgetcsv($handle);
    if (!$header) {
        fclose($handle);
        echo json_encode(['success' => false, 'error' => 'Empty CSV']);
        exit;
    }
    $header = array_map('trim', $header);
    if (isset($header[0])) {
        $header[0] = preg_replace('/^\xEF\xBB\xBF/', '', $header[0]);
    }
    $rows = [];
    while (($row = fgetcsv($handle)) !== false) {
        $row = array_slice(array_map('trim', $row), 0, count($header));
        $rows[] = array_combine($header, array_pad($row, count($header), ''));
    }
    fclose($handle);
    echo json_encode(['success' => true, 'header' => $header, 'rows' => $rows, 'total' => count($rows)]);
    exit;
}
```

This looks correct to me. `json_encode` on arrays of strings should work fine.

```
origin	https://github.com/leonardo-mdc/flashcards (fetch)
origin	https://github.com/leonardo-mdc/flashcards (push)
name: Deploy to InfinityFree

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # ── Main site ──────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Backup config.php (main)
        run: |
          curl -sS -u "${{ secrets.FTP_USERNAME }}:${{ secrets.FTP_PASSWORD }}" \
            "ftp://ftpupload.net/flashcards.my-board.org/htdocs/config.php" \
            -o /tmp/config-main.php.backup 2>/dev/null || \
            echo "No existing config.php (main)"

      - name: Sync files to main site
        uses: SamKirkland/FTP-Deploy-Action@v4.4.0
        with:
          server: ftpupload.net
          username: ${{ secrets.FTP_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.FTP_PASSWORD }}
          local-dir: ./
          server-dir: /flashcards.my-board.org/htdocs/
          exclude: |
            config.php
            .git*
          dangerous-clean-slate: false

      - name: Restore config.php (main)
        run: |
          if [ -f /tmp/config-main.php.backup ] && [ -s /tmp/config-main.php.backup ]; then
            curl -sS -u "${{ secrets.FTP_USERNAME }}:${{ secrets.FTP_PASSWORD }}" \
              -T /tmp/config-main.php.backup \
              "ftp://ftpupload.net/flashcards.my-board.org/htdocs/config.php"
            echo "config.php restored (main)"
          else
            echo "Upload config.php manually via InfinityFree file manager for main site"
          fi

      # ── Mirror site ─────────────────────────────────────────────
      - name: Backup config.php (mirror)
        run: |

```

Interesting! The Tailwind CDN script is at line 234, and the admin.css is at line 235. Tailwind CDN injects CSS dynamically (via `<style>` tag), so it might be injected AFTER admin.css loads, or the order depends on timing.

But the issue is:
- Tailwind's `.hidden` uses `display: none !important`  
- admin.css's `.modal-overlay` uses `display: flex`

When both are applied to the same element, `!important` wins regardless of source order. So `.hidden` would always override `.modal-overlay`.

When `.hidden` is REMOVED (via `classList.remove('hidden')`), only `.modal-overlay` with `display: flex` remains, and the modal becomes visible.

This logic is correct. The modal SHOULD work.

So the `importStepFile` and `importStepPreview` elements don't have `.modal-overlay` class - they're just divs. The Tailwind `.hidden` class with `display: none !important` would work on them when added, and when removed, their default display (which is `display: block` for divs) would apply. `importStepFile` is visible by default (no `hidden` class), and `importStepPreview` has `hidden` class by default.

When `loadCsvPreview` succeeds:
1. `importStepFile.classList.add('hidden')` → adds `hidden`, so `display: none !important`
2. `importStepPreview.classList.remove('hidden')` → removes `hidden`, so default `display: block`

This should work correctly.

```
384:     function renderDynamicFields(row) {
385:         const type = row.type || 'usage_cases';
386:         const cd = {};
387:         // Parse content_data if it exists as a JSON string
388:         let contentData = row.content_data;

390:             try { contentData = JSON.parse(contentData); } catch(e) { contentData = {}; }
391:         }
392:         if (!contentData || typeof contentData !== 'object') contentData = {};
393: 
394:         const def = contentData.definition || row.definition || '';
395:         const questionText = contentData.question_text || row.question_text || '';
396:         const sentence = contentData.sentence || row.sentence || '';
397:         const usage1 = contentData.usage1 || row.usage1 || '';
398:         const tip = contentData.tip || row.tip || '';
399:         const example = contentData.example || contentData.example1a || row.example1 || '';
400:         const correctAnswer = contentData.correct_answer || row.correct_answer || '';
401:         const options = (contentData.options || ['Option A', 'Option B', 'Option C']).join(', ');
402:         const correctIdx = contentData.correct_index !== undefined ? contentData.correct_index : 1;
403:         const imageUrl = contentData.image_url || row.image_url || '';
404:         const audioUrl = contentData.audio_url || row.audio_url || '';
405:         const description = contentData.description || row.description || '';
```

```
517:     function buildCsvContent() {
518:         const cols = ['set','set_id','type','title','level','definition','question_text','sentence','example1','example2','usage1','tip','correct_answer','explanation','image_url','audio_url','description','prompt','transcript'];
519:         const lines = [cols.join(',')];

521:             const vals = cols.map(col => {
522:                 let val = '';
523:                 if (col === 'set') val = row._setName || '';
524:                 else if (col === 'set_id') val = row.set_id || '';
525:                 else if (col === 'type') val = row.type || 'usage_cases';
526:                 else if (col === 'level') val = row.level || 'Beginner';
527:                 else if (col === 'title') val = row.title || '';
528:                 else if (col === 'definition') val = row.definition || '';
529:                 else if (col === 'question_text') val = row.question_text || '';
530:                 else if (col === 'sentence') val = row.sentence || '';
531:                 else if (col === 'example1') val = row.example1 || '';
532:                 else if (col === 'example2') val = row.example2 || '';
533:                 else if (col === 'usage1') val = row.usage1 || '';
534:                 else if (col === 'tip') val = row.tip || '';
535:                 else if (col === 'correct_answer') val = row.correct_answer || '';
536:                 else if (col === 'explanation') val = row.explanation || '';
537:                 else if (col === 'image_url') val = row.image_url || '';
538:                 else if (col === 'audio_url') val = row.audio_url || '';
539:                 else if (col === 'description') val = row.description || '';
540:                 else if (col === 'prompt') val = row.prompt || '';
541:                 else if (col === 'transcript') val = row.transcript || '';
542:                 else val = row[col] || '';
543:                 if (val.includes(',') || val.includes('"') || val.includes('\n')) val = '"' + val.replace(/"/g, '""') + '"';
544:                 return val;
545:             });
546:             lines.push(vals.join(','));
547:         });
548:         return lines.join('\n');
```

The editor fields are populated BEFORE `renderImportCardPreview()` is called. And `getImportContentData` reads from the editor fields. So the values will be up-to-date.

However, the `getImportContentData` function also reads from `document.getElementById('importEditDefinition')?.value` and `document.getElementById('importEditExtra')?.value`, which should be the same as `importEditDefinition.value` and `importEditExtra.value`. The difference is that `getImportContentData` uses `document.getElementById` instead of the cached variable. This should work fine but it's slightly less efficient. For clarity and consistency, I could use the cached variables. But it's not a bug, just a style issue. Let me leave it as is since it works.

Actually, now I realize there might be an issue. The `getImportContentData` function reads from `document.getElementById('importEditDefinition')?.value` and `document.getElementById('importEditExtra')?.value` at the TOP of the function:

```js
function getImportContentData(row) {
    const type = row.type || 'usage_cases';
    const definition = (document.getElementById('importEditDefinition')?.value || row.definition || '').trim();
    const extra = (document.getElementById('importEditExtra')?.value || '').trim();
    ...
}
```

But when called from `renderImportCardPreview`, the `row` parameter is the original row data. However, the user might have changed the fields in the editor without applying them. So:

- `row.title` vs `importEditTitle.value` → need to use the editor value
- `row.type` vs `importEditStyle.value` → need to use the editor value

In `renderImportCardPreview`, I have:
```js
const title = document.getElementById('importEditTitle')?.value || row.title || 'Untitled';
const type = document.getElementById('importEditStyle')?.value || row.type || 'usage_cases';
const level = document.getElementById('importEditLevel')?.value || row.level || 'Beginner';
const contentData = getImportContentData(row);
```

And in `getImportContentData`, I read from `document.getElementById`:
```js
const definition = (document.getElementById('importEditDefinition')?.value || row.definition || '').trim();
const extra = (document.getElementById('importEditExtra')?.value || '').trim();
```

So both the title/type/level and the content data fields are read from the editor (with fallback to row data). This is correct.

But WAIT - the `getImportContentData` function uses `row.type` (original row type) for its logic:
```js
const type = row.type || 'usage_cases';
```

But the type might have changed in the editor. If the user changes the type from "usage_cases" to "multiple_choice" in the editor, `row.type` is still "usage_cases" but `importEditStyle.value` is "multiple_choice".

In `renderImportCardPreview`:
```js
const type = document.getElementById('importEditStyle')?.value || row.type || 'usage_cases';
```

So `type` is correctly the editor value. But then `getImportContentData(row)` is called with the ORIGINAL row, and inside, it uses `row.type`:
```js
const type = row.type || 'usage_cases';
```

This is WRONG! If the user changed the type, `getImportContentData` would use the OLD type.

I need to fix this. The `getImportContentData` should also use the editor value for type. Let me update it to accept the type parameter:

</details>

```
## 2. JavaScript Functions for User Editing in `admin.js`

**File:** `/home/leonardo/Documents/Personal/Inglês/Aulas/materialLeo/ankiCards/flashcards/assets/js/admin.js`

### `showUserManagement()` and `loadUsers()` (lines 581-600)

```
581:     function showUserManagement() {
582:         if (cardEditorSection) cardEditorSection.classList.add('hidden');
583:         if (userManagementSection) userManagementSection.classList.remove('hidden');
584:         loadUsers();
585:     }
586: 
587:     async function loadUsers() {
588:         if (!userListContainer) return;
589:         userListContainer.innerHTML = '<div class="text-center py-4"><div class="loader"></div> Loading users...</div>';
590: 
591:         const response = await fetch('admin_cards.php?action=get_users&t=' + Date.now(), {
592:             headers: { 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' }
593:         });
594:         const data = await response.json();
595:         if (data.success && data.users) {
596:             renderUsers(data.users);
597:         } else {
598:             userListContainer.innerHTML = '<div class="text-center text-red-500 py-4">Error loading users</div>';
599:         }
600:     }
```

### `renderUsers()` -- includes the "Edit" button with `data-level` attribute (lines 602-668)

This function renders the user table and attaches click handlers for edit, delete, and reset buttons. The **edit-user-btn** passes all user data via `data-*` attributes, including `data-level`.

```
602:     function renderUsers(users) {
603:         if (!users.length) {
604:             userListContainer.innerHTML = '<div class="text-center text-gray-500 py-4">No users found</div>';
605:             return;
606:         }
607: 
608:         let html = '<table class="w-full" style="border-collapse:collapse;">';
609:         html += '<tr><th style="text-align:left;padding:8px;border-bottom:2px solid #e2e8f0;">Name</th><th style="text-align:left;padding:8px;border-bottom:2px solid #e2e8f0;">Username</th><th style="text-align:left;padding:8px;border-bottom:2px solid #e2e8f0;">Role</th><th style="text-align:left;padding:8px;border-bottom:2px solid #e2e8f0;">Progress</th><th style="text-align:left;padding:8px;border-bottom:2px solid #e2e8f0;">Level</th><th style="text-align:left;padding:8px;border-bottom:2px solid #e2e8f0;">Actions</th></tr>';
610:         users.forEach(user => {
611:             html += `<tr>
612:                 <td style="padding:8px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;">${escapeHtml(user.full_name || user.username)}</td>
613:                 <td style="padding:8px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;">${escapeHtml(user.username)}</td>
614:                 <td style="padding:8px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;">${user.is_admin ? '<span class="card-type mcq">Admin</span>' : '<span class="card-type text">Student</span>'}</td>
615:                 <td style="padding:8px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;">${user.progress || 0}%</td>
616:                 <td style="padding:8px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;">${escapeHtml(user.english_level || 'Beginner')}</td>
617:                 <td style="padding:8px;border-bottom:1px solid #e2e8f0;">
618:                     <button class="edit-user-btn btn btn-primary text-xs" data-id="${user.id}" data-username="${escapeHtml(user.username)}" data-fullname="${escapeHtml(user.full_name || '')}" data-level="${escapeHtml(user.english_level || 'Beginner')}" data-admin="${user.is_admin}" style="padding:4px 10px;font-size:0.7rem;">✏️ Edit</button>
619:                     <button class="reset-user-btn btn btn-warning text-xs" data-id="${user.id}" style="padding:4px 10px;font-size:0.7rem;">🔄 Reset</button>
620:                     <button class="delete-user-btn btn btn-danger text-xs" data-id="${user.id}" style="padding:4px 10px;font-size:0.7rem;">🗑 Delete</button>
621:                 </td>
622:             </tr>`;
623:         });
624:         html += '</table>';
625: 
626:         userListContainer.innerHTML = html;
627: 
628:         document.querySelectorAll('.edit-user-btn').forEach(btn => {
629:             btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
630:                 openEditUserModal(btn.dataset);
631:             });
632:         });
633: 
634:         document.querySelectorAll('.delete-user-btn').forEach(btn => {
635:             btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
636:                 const userId = parseInt(btn.dataset.id);
637:                 if (confirm('Delete this user? This action cannot be undone.')) {
638:                     const response = await fetch(`admin_cards.php?action=delete_user&user_id=${userId}`, {
639:                         headers: { 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' }

641:                     const result = await response.json();

644:                     } else {
645:                         alert(result.error || 'Error deleting user');
646:                     }
647:                 }
648:             });
649:         });
650: 
651:         document.querySelectorAll('.reset-user-btn').forEach(btn => {
652:             btn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
653:                 const userId = parseInt(btn.dataset.id);
654:                 if (confirm('Reset all progress for this user? This will clear review history and progress.')) {
655:                     const response = await fetch(`admin_cards.php?action=reset_user_progress&user_id=${userId}`, {
656:                         headers: { 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' }
657:                     });
658:                     const result = await response.json();
659:                     if (result.success) {
660:                         alert('Progress reset successfully');
661:                         loadUsers();
662:                     } else {
663:                         alert(result.error || 'Error resetting progress');
664:                     }
665:                 }
666:             });
667:         });
668:     }
```

### `openEditUserModal()` -- the edit user modal (dynamically created) (lines 670-764)

This is where the **edit user dialog HTML** lives, including the **level field**. It also populates the level via `data.level`, and handles saving.

```
670:     function openEditUserModal(data) {
671:         const existing = document.getElementById('editUserModal');
672:         if (existing) existing.remove();
673: 
674:         const modal = document.createElement('div');
675:         modal.id = 'editUserModal';
676:         modal.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;inset:0;background:rgba(0,0,0,0.5);display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;z-index:1000;';
677:         modal.innerHTML = `
678:             <div class="whiteboard-card" style="max-width:500px;width:90%;padding:24px;max-height:80vh;overflow-y:auto;">
679:                 <h3 class="text-lg marker-underline mb-3">✏️ Edit User</h3>
680:                 <input type="hidden" id="editUserId" value="${data.id}">
681:                 <label class="block font-bold mb-1">Username:</label>
682:                 <input type="text" id="editUserUsername" class="form-input" value="${data.username}" maxlength="30">
683:                 <label class="block font-bold mb-1">Full Name:</label>
684:                 <input type="text" id="editUserFullName" class="form-input" value="${data.fullname}">
685:                 <label class="block font-bold mb-1">English Level:</label>
686:                 <select id="editUserLevel" class="form-select">
687:                     <option value="Beginner" ${data.level === 'Beginner' ? 'selected' : ''}>🔰 Beginner</option>
688:                     <option value="Intermediate" ${data.level === 'Intermediate' ? 'selected' : ''}>📚 Intermediate</option>
689:                     <option value="Advanced" ${data.level === 'Advanced' ? 'selected' : ''}>🎓 Advanced</option>
690:                 </select>
691:                 <label class="flex items-center gap-2 my-2">
692:                     <input type="checkbox" id="editUserIsAdmin" value="1" ${data.admin === 'true' || data.admin === '1' ? 'checked' : ''}>
693:                     <span class="font-bold">Admin privileges</span>
694:                 </label>
695:                 <div class="mt-3 mb-2">
696:                     <label class="block font-bold mb-1">📚 Visible Card Sets:</label>
697:                     <p class="text-xs text-gray-500 mb-2">Leave all unchecked to show all sets</p>
698:                     <div id="userSetAccessContainer" class="space-y-1">
699:                         <div class="text-sm text-gray-400">Loading sets...</div>
700:                     </div>
701:                 </div>
702:                 <div class="flex gap-2 mt-3">
703:                     <button id="saveEditUserBtn" class="btn btn-success flex-1">💾 Save</button>
704:                     <button id="cancelEditUserBtn" class="btn btn-secondary flex-1">Cancel</button>
705:                 </div>
706:                 <p id="editUserError" class="text-red-600 text-center mt-2 hidden"></p>
707:             </div>
708:         `;
709:         document.body.appendChild(modal);
710: 
711:         // Load card sets and user's current access
712:         (async () => {
713:             const [setsRes, accessRes] = await Promise.all([
714:                 fetch('admin_cards.php?action=get_sets&t=' + Date.now(), { headers: { 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' } }),
715:                 fetch(`admin_cards.php?action=get_user_sets&user_id=${data.id}&t=${Date.now()}`, { headers: { 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' } })
716:             ]);
717:             const setsData = await setsRes.json();
718:             const accessData = await accessRes.json();
719:             const allSets = setsData.success ? setsData.sets : [];
720:             const userSetIds = accessData.success ? accessData.set_ids : [];
721:             const container = document.getElementById('userSetAccessContainer');
722:             if (allSets.length === 0) {
723:                 container.innerHTML = '<div class="text-sm text-gray-400">No card sets available</div>';
724:             } else {
725:                 container.innerHTML = allSets.map(set => `
726:                     <label class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm cursor-pointer">
727:                         <input type="checkbox" class="user-set-checkbox" value="${set.id}" ${userSetIds.includes(set.id) ? 'checked' : ''}>
728:                         ${escapeHtml(set.name)}
729:                     </label>
730:                 `).join('');
731:             }
732:         })();
733: 
734:         document.getElementById('cancelEditUserBtn').addEventListener('click', () => modal.remove());
735:         modal.addEventListener('click', (e) => { if (e.target === modal) modal.remove(); });
736: 
737:         document.getElementById('saveEditUserBtn').addEventListener('click', async () => {
738:             const id = parseInt(document.getElementById('editUserId').value);
739:             const username = document.getElementById('editUserUsername').value.trim();
740:             const fullName = document.getElementById('editUserFullName').value.trim();
741:             const englishLevel = document.getElementById('editUserLevel').value;
742:             const isAdmin = document.getElementById('editUserIsAdmin').checked;
743: 
744:             if (!username) { alert('Username is required'); return; }
745: 
746:             const response = await fetch('admin_cards.php?action=update_user', {
747:                 method: 'POST',
748:                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' },
749:                 body: JSON.stringify({ id, username, full_name: fullName, english_level: englishLevel, is_admin: isAdmin })
750:             });
751:             const result = await response.json();
752:             if (!result.success) { alert(result.error || 'Error saving user'); return; }
753: 
754:             const checkedSets = [...document.querySelectorAll('.user-set-checkbox:checked')].map(cb => parseInt(cb.value));
755:             await fetch('admin_cards.php?action=set_user_sets', {
756:                 method: 'POST',
757:                 headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', 'X-Requested-With': 'XMLHttpRequest' },
758:                 body: JSON.stringify({ user_id: id, set_ids: checkedSets })
759:             });
760: 
761:             modal.remove();
762:             loadUsers();
763:         });
764:     }
```

## Summary

| Aspect | File | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| **Add User form HTML** (with level `<select>`) | `admin_cards.php` | 371-391 |
| **User list container** (placeholder for dynamic table) | `admin_cards.php` | 393-400 |
| **`showUserManagement()`** -- toggles visibility | `admin.js` | 581-585 |
| **`loadUsers()`** -- fetches user list from server | `admin.js` | 587-600 |
| **`renderUsers()`** -- renders user table, creates **edit-user-btn** with `data-level`, attaches click handlers | `admin.js` | 602-668 |
| **`openEditUserModal(data)`** -- dynamically creates the edit user modal with **level `<select>`** populated from `data.level`, loads card set checkboxes, and the **save handler** that reads `editUserLevel.value` and sends it in the update request | `admin.js` | 670-764 |
| **Button wiring** (`toggleUsersBtn`, `backToCardsBtn`, `createUserBtn`) | `admin.js` | 827-829 |

**Key observations about the level field in the edit modal:**

- The level `<select>` is defined at lines 686-690 of `admin.js` with three options: `Beginner`, `Intermediate`, `Advanced`.
- The currently selected option is set via `data.level` (from the `data-level` attribute of the clicked edit button), using inline ternary: `${data.level === 'Beginner' ? 'selected' : ''}` etc.
- On save (line 741), the level is read with `document.getElementById('editUserLevel').value` and sent as `english_level` in the JSON payload to `admin_cards.php?action=update_user` (line 749).
</task_result>
</task>
```

Let me plan the changes:

### User.php changes
Modify `update()` to accept an optional password:
```php
public static function update(int $id, string $username, string $fullName, string $englishLevel, bool $isAdmin, ?string $password = null): void
{
    self::ensureTable();
    $pdo = Database::getConnection();
    if ($password !== null && $password !== '') {
        $hash = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
        $stmt = $pdo->prepare("UPDATE users SET username = ?, full_name = ?, english_level = ?, is_admin = ?, password = ? WHERE id = ?");
        $stmt->execute([$username, $fullName, $englishLevel, $isAdmin ? 1 : 0, $hash, $id]);
    } else {
        $stmt = $pdo->prepare("UPDATE users SET username = ?, full_name = ?, english_level = ?, is_admin = ? WHERE id = ?");
        $stmt->execute([$username, $fullName, $englishLevel, $isAdmin ? 1 : 0, $id]);
    }
}
```

### admin_cards.php changes
In the `update_user` action, extract password from the request and pass it to `User::update()`:
```php
$password = $data['password'] ?? '';
User::update($userId, $username, $fullName, $englishLevel, $isAdmin, $password);
```

### admin.js changes
In `openEditUserModal()`:
- Remove the level `<select>` (lines 685-690)
- Replace with two password fields
- Update save handler to handle passwords

In `renderUsers()`:
- Optionally remove `data-level` from the edit button (but it won't hurt to keep it)

It sends `english_level`. Since we're removing the level from the edit modal, we still need to send the existing level. But we don't have it available anymore (we removed the select).


So `data.level` contains the current level. I can keep the `data-level` attribute and use `data.level` as a hidden field value.

But actually, looking at the PHP `User::update` method, `english_level` is required. So I need to send it. I'll just store the existing level in a hidden field in the modal.

Alternatively, I can just keep the `data-level` on the button and reference it in the save handler. But since the modal is dynamically created, I could store it in a hidden field.

```
5:     public static function ensureTable(): void

27:         }

30:         if (!$stmt->fetch()) {
31:             $pdo->exec("ALTER TABLE user_card_progress CHANGE student_id user_id INT NOT NULL");
32:         }
33:     }
34: 
35:     public static function ensureHistoryTable(): void
36:     {
37:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
38:         $pdo->exec("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS review_history (
39:             id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
40:             user_id INT NOT NULL,
41:             card_id INT NOT NULL,
42:             quality INT NOT NULL,
43:             was_correct TINYINT(1) DEFAULT 0,
44:             reviewed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
45:             FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
46:             FOREIGN KEY (card_id) REFERENCES cards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
47:         )");
48:     }
49: 
50:     public static function record(int $cardId, int $userId, int $quality, bool $wasCorrect): void
51:     {
52:         self::ensureTable();
53:         self::ensureHistoryTable();
54:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
55: 
56:         $daysToAdd = match ($quality) {
57:             0 => 1,
58:             2 => 3,
59:             3 => 7,
60:             default => 1,
61:         };
62: 
63:         $nextReview = date('Y-m-d', strtotime("+$daysToAdd days"));
64:         $today = date('Y-m-d');
65: 
66:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT id FROM user_card_progress WHERE user_id = ? AND card_id = ?");
67:         $stmt->execute([$userId, $cardId]);
68:         $exists = $stmt->fetch();
69: 
70:         if ($exists) {
71:             $stmt = $pdo->prepare("
72:                 UPDATE user_card_progress
73:                 SET next_review = ?, last_review = ?, was_correct = ?, total_reviews = total_reviews + 1
74:                 WHERE user_id = ? AND card_id = ?
75:             ");
76:             $stmt->execute([$nextReview, $today, $wasCorrect ? 1 : 0, $userId, $cardId]);
77:         } else {
78:             $stmt = $pdo->prepare("
79:                 INSERT INTO user_card_progress (user_id, card_id, next_review, last_review, was_correct, total_reviews)
80:                 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 1)
81:             ");
82:             $stmt->execute([$userId, $cardId, $nextReview, $today, $wasCorrect ? 1 : 0]);
83:         }
84: 
85:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("INSERT INTO review_history (user_id, card_id, quality, was_correct) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)");
86:         $stmt->execute([$userId, $cardId, $quality, $wasCorrect ? 1 : 0]);
87:     }
88: 
89:     public static function resetForUser(int $userId): void
90:     {
91:         self::ensureTable();
92:         self::ensureHistoryTable();
93:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
94:         $pdo->prepare("DELETE FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?")->execute([$userId]);
95:         $pdo->prepare("DELETE FROM user_card_progress WHERE user_id = ?")->execute([$userId]);
96:     }
97: 
98:     public static function ensureSetAccessTable(): void
99:     {
100:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
101:         $pdo->exec("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS student_set_access (
102:             user_id INT NOT NULL,
103:             set_id INT NOT NULL,
104:             PRIMARY KEY (user_id, set_id),
105:             FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
106:             FOREIGN KEY (set_id) REFERENCES card_sets(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
107:         )");
108:     }
109: 
110:     public static function getAccessibleSets(int $userId): array
111:     {
112:         self::ensureSetAccessTable();
113:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
114:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT set_id FROM student_set_access WHERE user_id = ?");
115:         $stmt->execute([$userId]);
116:         return $stmt->fetchAll(\PDO::FETCH_COLUMN);
117:     }
118: 
119:     public static function setAccessibleSets(int $userId, array $setIds): void
120:     {
121:         self::ensureSetAccessTable();
122:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
123:         $pdo->prepare("DELETE FROM student_set_access WHERE user_id = ?")->execute([$userId]);
124:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("INSERT INTO student_set_access (user_id, set_id) VALUES (?, ?)");
125:         foreach ($setIds as $setId) {
126:             $stmt->execute([$userId, (int) $setId]);
127:         }
128:     }
129: 
130:     public static function hasAccessToSet(int $userId, int $setId): bool
131:     {
132:         $sets = self::getAccessibleSets($userId);
133:         if (empty($sets)) return true;
134:         return in_array($setId, $sets);
135:     }
136: 
137:     public static function isWithin30DayCycle(int $userId): bool
138:     {
139:         self::ensureHistoryTable();
140:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
141:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT MIN(reviewed_at) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?");
142:         $stmt->execute([$userId]);
143:         $firstReview = $stmt->fetchColumn();
144:         if (!$firstReview) return false;
145:         $daysSinceFirst = (new \DateTime())->diff(new \DateTime($firstReview))->days;
146:         return $daysSinceFirst < 30;
147:     }
148: 
149:     public static function checkAndResetCycle(int $userId): bool
150:     {
151:         if (!self::isWithin30DayCycle($userId)) {
152:             $stats = self::getStats($userId);
153:             if ($stats['total_reviews'] > 0) {
154:                 self::resetForUser($userId);
155:                 return true;
156:             }
157:         }
158:         return false;
159:     }
160: 
161:     public static function getStats(int $userId): array
162:     {
163:         self::ensureHistoryTable();
164:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
165: 
166:         $total = $pdo->prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?");

168:         $totalReviews = (int) $total->fetchColumn();
169:

171:         $correct->execute([$userId]);
172:         $correctCount = (int) $correct->fetchColumn();
173: 
174:         $distinctCards = $pdo->prepare("SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT card_id) FROM review_history WHERE user_id = ?");
175:         $distinctCards->execute([$userId]);
176:         $cardsReviewed = (int) $distinctCards->fetchColumn();
177: 
178:         $daily = $pdo->prepare("
179:             SELECT DATE(reviewed_at) as day, COUNT(*) as count
180:             FROM review_history
181:             WHERE user_id = ? AND reviewed_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 30 DAY)
182:             GROUP BY DATE(reviewed_at)
183:             ORDER BY day ASC
184:         ");
185:         $daily->execute([$userId]);
186:         $dailyStats = $daily->fetchAll();
187: 
188:         $upcoming = $pdo->prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_card_progress WHERE user_id = ? AND next_review <= CURDATE()");
189:         $upcoming->execute([$userId]);
190:         $dueToday = (int) $upcoming->fetchColumn();
191: 
192:         return [
193:             'total_reviews' => $totalReviews,
194:             'correct_count' => $correctCount,
195:             'incorrect_count' => $totalReviews - $correctCount,
196:             'cards_reviewed' => $cardsReviewed,
197:             'daily' => $dailyStats,
198:             'due_today' => $dueToday,
199:         ];
200:     }
201: }

(End of file - total 201 lines)
</content>
```

```
1: <?php
2: 
3: class Card
4: {
5:     public static function getBySetAndLevels(
6:         ?int $setId,
7:         array $levels,
8:         bool $randomMode = false,
9:         int $limit = 500,
10:         ?int $excludeUserId = null,
11:         ?array $setIds = null
12:     ): array {
13:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
14:         $sql = "SELECT c.id, c.set_id, c.title, c.pattern_type, c.level, c.question_text, c.content_data, s.name AS set_name FROM cards c LEFT JOIN card_sets s ON c.set_id = s.id WHERE 1=1";
15:         $params = [];
16: 
17:         if (!$randomMode && $setId !== null && $setId > 0) {
18:             $sql .= " AND c.set_id = ?";
19:             $params[] = $setId;
20:         } elseif (!$randomMode && ($setId === null || $setId === 0)) {
21:             $sql .= " AND c.set_id = 1";
22:         }
23: 
24:         if ($randomMode && !empty($setIds)) {
25:             $placeholders = implode(',', array_fill(0, count($setIds), '?'));
26:             $sql .= " AND c.set_id IN ($placeholders)";
27:             foreach ($setIds as $sid) {
28:                 $params[] = (int) $sid;
29:             }
30:         }
31: 
32:         if (!empty($levels)) {
33:             $placeholders = implode(',', array_fill(0, count($levels), '?'));
34:             $sql .= " AND c.level IN ($placeholders)";
35:             foreach ($levels as $level) {
36:                 $params[] = $level;
37:             }
38:         }
39: 
40:         if ($excludeUserId !== null && $excludeUserId > 0) {
41:             $sql .= " AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT card_id FROM user_card_progress WHERE user_id = ? AND next_review > CURDATE())";
42:             $params[] = $excludeUserId;
43:         }
44: 
45:         $sql .= " ORDER BY c.id ASC LIMIT " . (int) $limit;
46: 
47:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare($sql);
48:         $stmt->execute($params);
49:         $cards = $stmt->fetchAll();
50: 
51:         foreach ($cards as &$card) {
52:             if (!empty($card['content_data'])) {
53:                 $decoded = json_decode($card['content_data'], true);
54:                 $card['content_data'] = $decoded ?: [];
55:             } else {
56:                 $card['content_data'] = [];
57:             }
58:         }
59: 
60:         return $cards;
61:     }
62: 
63:     public static function getBySet(int $setId): array
64:     {
65:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
66:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT id, set_id, title, pattern_type, level, question_text, content_data FROM cards WHERE set_id = ? ORDER BY id");
67:         $stmt->execute([$setId]);
68:         return $stmt->fetchAll();
69:     }
70: 
71:     public static function getById(int $id): ?array
72:     {
73:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
74:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("SELECT * FROM cards WHERE id = ?");
75:         $stmt->execute([$id]);

77:         return $row ?: null;

82:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
83:         $id = (int) ($data['id'] ?? 0);

87:         $level = $data['level'] ?? 'Beginner';

90: 
91:         if ($id > 0) {
92:             $stmt = $pdo->prepare("UPDATE cards SET set_id=?, title=?, pattern_type=?, level=?, question_text=?, content_data=? WHERE id=?");
93:             $stmt->execute([$setId, $title, $patternType, $level, $questionText, $contentData, $id]);
94:             return $id;
95:         } else {
96:             $stmt = $pdo->prepare("INSERT INTO cards (set_id, title, pattern_type, level, question_text, content_data) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?)");
97:             $stmt->execute([$setId, $title, $patternType, $level, $questionText, $contentData]);
98:             return (int) $pdo->lastInsertId();
99:         }
100:     }
101:

104:         $pdo = Database::getConnection();
105:         $stmt = $pdo->prepare("DELETE FROM cards WHERE id = ?");
106:         $stmt->execute([$id]);
107:     }
108: }
```

```
129:     let statusTimeout;
130:     function displayStatusMessage(message, type = 'info') {

162:                     </div>
163:                     <div class="marker-border p-3 md:p-4 bg-white/80">
164:                         <label class="text-lg md:text-xl font-bold text-gray-800 title-font">🔒 Password</label>
165:                         <input type="password" id="authPasswordInput" placeholder="Enter password" class="w-full p-2 md:p-2 text-base md:text-lg border-2 rounded-xl mt-1">
166:                     </div>
167:                     <button id="loginBtn" class="w-full bg-blue-700 text-white py-2 md:py-3 text-lg md:text-xl title-font rounded-xl shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,0.2)] hover:translate-y-1 transition-all">🔑 LOG IN</button>
168:                     <p id="authError" class="text-red-600 text-center hidden"></p>
169:                 </div>

172: 
173:         const showError = (msg) => {
174:             const el = document.getElementById('authError');

178:         document.getElementById('loginBtn').addEventListener('click', async () => {
```
